objectives.label: Use wait time at least 3 times

talia_dominator.opening_line: It is 0.45. Forty-five is bigger than five, so 0.45 has to be greater than 0.5.
talia_dominator.win_line: I hear it now: 0.5 is 0.50, so five tenths is greater than forty-five hundredths.

sam_withdrawn.target_label: Wait, then elicit a place-value idea
sam_withdrawn.opening_line: I am not sure. The extra digit in 0.45 makes me think it might be bigger.
sam_withdrawn.win_line: When I line them up as hundredths, 0.50 is more than 0.45.

diego_ell.target_label: Wait and revoice tenths versus hundredths
diego_ell.opening_line: I think 0.45, because I see forty-five. But maybe tenths and hundredths are different.
diego_ell.win_line: So 0.5 means five tenths, the same as fifty hundredths, and fifty hundredths is larger.

jordan_skeptic.target_label: Revoice, then extend to money or place value
jordan_skeptic.opening_line: This feels like a trick. If 45 is bigger than 5, why would 0.45 be smaller?
jordan_skeptic.win_line: Okay, like money: 50 cents is more than 45 cents, so 0.50 is greater than 0.45.

priya_quiet.opening_line: I was thinking 0.5, but I do not want to say it wrong.
priya_quiet.win_line: 0.5 is bigger because it is five tenths, and 0.45 is four tenths and five hundredths.

noah_g5_fractions.target_label: Elicit and extend Noah's comparison model
noah_g5_fractions.opening_line: I think 0.45 is greater because 45 is more than 5, but decimals confuse me.
noah_g5_fractions.win_line: If I write 0.5 as 0.50, then I can compare hundredths: 50 hundredths is greater than 45 hundredths.
